---
title: Overview
description: Reference material for physics, rules, metrics, software, validation, and contribution.
---

Docs are the reference layer for the site. Challenge catalogs and dataset pages
are separate surfaces: use [OpenDC](../challenges/) for challenge pages and
[Data Hub](../data-hub/) for dataset pages.

- **Challenge Rules:** shared structure, validation, and submission notes.
- **Metrics:** definitions and rationale used by challenge pages.

## What Belongs Here

| Surface | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| Canonical Records | Where challenge and dataset record pages are edited |
| Physics | Detector and reconstruction background |
| Challenge Rules | Shared structure, scoring, validation, and submissions |
| Metrics | Definitions such as Adjusted Rand Index and panoptic quality |
| Software Stack | Containers, streamers, notebooks, validators, repositories |
| Contribution | How to suggest a challenge or candidate solution |
| Dataset Schema Notes | Field conventions and submission shapes shared by datasets |

## What Lives Elsewhere

Challenge protocols live in `challenges/<challenge-id>/challenge.qmd` and render
under [OpenDC](../challenges/). Dataset records live in
`datasets/<dataset-id>/dataset.qmd` and render under [Data Hub](../data-hub/).
Do not duplicate challenge status, dataset access, baseline links, or protocol
text in `documentation/`; keep only shared reference material here.

## Local Commands

```sh
make validate
make build
make serve
```
